Class ModelController

java.lang.Object
com.example.passmanager.ModelController
public class ModelController extends Object
This controller is used to handle account creation, logging in, and successful login attempts vs unsuccessful

  • Method Details

    • onLoginClick

      public void onLoginClick(javafx.event.ActionEvent event) throws IOException
      Navigates to the login view.
      Parameters:
      event - the action event from the Login button
      Throws:
      IOException - if the login-view FXML cannot be loaded

    • onCreateAccountClick

      public void onCreateAccountClick(javafx.event.ActionEvent event) throws IOException
      Navigates to the create-account form view.
      Parameters:
      event - the action event from the Create Account button
      Throws:
      IOException - if the create-account FXML cannot be loaded

    • onCreateAccountSubmit

      public void onCreateAccountSubmit(javafx.event.ActionEvent event) throws Exception
      Handles submission of the create-account form.

      Validates that all fields are filled and passwords match, derives an AES key, encrypts the password, checks for duplicate accounts, persists the new account to the database, and redirects to the login view on success.

      Parameters:
      event - the action event from the Submit button
      Throws:
      Exception - if encryption, key derivation, or database access fails

    • onLoginSubmit

      protected void onLoginSubmit(javafx.event.ActionEvent event) throws Exception
      Handles login form submission. Looks up the stored encrypted password and salt for the username, re-creates the AES key from the entered password, decrypts the stored password, and compares it. On success, stores the key in the Session class method setKey and navigates to the main screen.
      Parameters:
      event - the action event from the Login button
      Throws:
      Exception - if decryption, key derivation, or database access fails

    • onBackClick

      protected void onBackClick(javafx.event.ActionEvent event) throws IOException
      Navigates back to the welcome screen.
      Parameters:
      event - the action event from the Back button
      Throws:
      IOException - if the first-scene FXML cannot be loaded
